sections.heading: Follow-Up
sections.content: After completion of curative-intent treatment, follow-up is based on the Society of Gynecologic Oncology (SGO) recommendations, tailored to recurrence risk and patient preference [42]. The primary goal of surveillance is the early detection of recurrent or metastatic disease, as some recurrences may be amenable to curative-intent salvage therapy. Patient education about symptoms of recurrence (e.g., vaginal discharge, bleeding, pain) is emphasized. While routine imaging and cytology have limited value in detecting asymptomatic recurrences, they are used selectively based on stage and risk [42,43].

timeline.title: First 2-3 Years Post-Treatment
timeline.body: Most recurrences occur within the first 2-3 years post-treatment. Patterns include: 1) Local/pelvic recurrence (most common), 2) Distant metastases (e.g., lungs, liver, bone, para-aortic nodes), 3) Combined local and distant recurrence. The location of recurrence influences salvage therapy options (e.g., pelvic exenteration for central pelvic recurrence) [46,47].

late_effects.name: Genitourinary symptoms
late_effects.detail: Screen for genitourinary symptoms: dysuria, frequency, incontinence [44]

late_effects.name: Gastrointestinal symptoms
late_effects.detail: Screen for gastrointestinal symptoms: chronic diarrhea, rectal bleeding [44]

late_effects.name: Sexual dysfunction
late_effects.detail: Assess for sexual dysfunction: vaginal dryness, stenosis, dyspareunia [44,45]

late_effects.name: Lymphedema
late_effects.detail: Evaluate for lymphedema of lower extremities [44]

late_effects.name: Bone density loss
late_effects.detail: Screen for bone density loss, especially post-oophorectomy or pelvic RT [44]

late_effects.name: Psychosocial issues
late_effects.detail: Assess for psychosocial issues: depression, anxiety, fear of recurrence [40]

late_effects.name: Healthy lifestyle counseling
late_effects.detail: Provide counseling on healthy lifestyle, smoking cessation, and obesity management [44]

late_effects.name: Second cancer risks
late_effects.detail: Discuss second cancer risks (e.g., colon, bladder) relevant to prior RT fields [36]
